title: Terahertz Imaging (0.1-10 THz time-domain / CW)
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence template (fan-out artifact). Physics below is grounded in canonical NDT/NDE literature for this principle.

E.description: Terahertz Imaging (0.1-10 THz time-domain / CW): terahertz time domain produces the measurement through a 4-node primitive DAG L.thz_source -> L.transmit_sample -> D.thz_detector -> int.spectral, with spectral-channel integration and additive Gaussian thermal/electronic noise. Recovery is posed as a linear inverse problem that inverts the forward operator to estimate the scene-side 2D complex permittivity. Difficulty tier delta=5 with effective condition number kappa_eff~14; calibration-level mismatch (water_absorption, scattering_at_defects, dispersive_media) sets the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form imaging equation.
E.forward_model: E(t, x, y) = source(t) * propagator(epsilon_r(x,y,omega), d) + n; invert for epsilon_r
E.world_state_x: Defect / strain / composition map depending on principle

W.regime: Existence of the recovered 2D complex permittivity is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by the declared priors. Stability is moderately conditioned (kappa_eff ~= 14); water_absorption dominates the stability cliff; scattering_at_defects and the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Additive gaussian thermal/electronic noise sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor, while mild Tikhonov or analytic inversion is sufficient at the nominal Omega point.

C.solver_class: linear-operator + convex optimisation [Fabry-Perot-Analysis, TV-ADMM-THz] | linear-operator + deep neural prior [THz-Net]
C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: ||x_hat - x||_2 <= C1 * sigma_noise / sqrt(N_measurements). Mismatch-induced bias adds + C2 * |water_absorption| + C3 * ||x - x_s||_1 (best s-sparse / low-rank approximation residual). Iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the MLE; epsilon_fn captures the monotone quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(H * W * N_spectral_bins * log(...)) per iteration; learned variants: O(H W Z * F_theta_cost) per forward pass
